key_facts: TCL-incubated industrial AI company founded 2018 in Shenzhen
Launched dedicated PV smart monitoring and O&M platform
Platform covers: real-time analytics, predictive fault detection, digital-twin power station management
Series B: hundreds of millions CNY (2022, led by SAIC Capital)
Series C: hundreds of millions CNY (2025, Optical Valley Finance)
HKU (Hong Kong University) joint AI lab established April 2025

funding_history.notes: Early growth capital following TCL incubation
Hundreds of millions CNY; SAIC = Shanghai Automotive Industry Corp, signals auto-energy convergence interest
Late-stage growth; HKU AI lab established same year

business_model_notes: Industrial AI platform pivoting into solar O&M. Getech's core competency is AI-driven predictive maintenance for manufacturing (TCL factories); the solar O&M product extends this capability to distributed PV assets. Digital-twin model: create a virtual replica of each power station for predictive failure analysis. Revenue: B2B SaaS license + data analytics services. SAIC investment suggests interest in solar O&M for automotive-adjacent (factory rooftop, EV charging integration).
competitive_position: AI-differentiated O&M platform competing with simpler monitoring tools. Getech's industrial AI pedigree (from TCL manufacturing) gives it credibility for large enterprise solar portfolios. Main competition: Guoneng Rixin (301162.SZ, forecasting-focused) and SOLARMAN (scale player). Revenue of CNY 1B+ puts it in the same tier as Guoneng Rixin (CNY 550M in 2024).
research_notes: Medium confidence. Series A and revenue data from 36Kr article on Getech. SAIC investment confirmed via news sources. HKU AI lab from company press release. Revenue of 'exceeded CNY 1B' from company disclosure — year unclear (likely 2024). Getech's connection to TCL's supply chain makes it a strategic solar tech company even without standalone market leadership.
